other_observers: Peter Gent, Rachel Hopper, at least a dozen others; I was in the initial group that spotted the bird, but I believe that someone else (perhaps Peter Gent) was the first person to identify it.
description: This bird was of similar size and shape to a MacGillivray's Warbler (one of which was nearby).   Underparts: there was a solid gray hood with blackish at the bottom of the hood (i.e. on the throat), just before the sharp transition to a fairly bright yellow breast and belly. the tail was short.  I saw the face well, but briefly, several times in good light. It was a dark gray, with no white eye markings at all.  The back and wings were an unmarked greenish yellow.Behaviors: It was foraging in foliage, 5' to 10' above the ground.Call: no vocalization heardPlumage: breeding plumage
